tasks.title: Create project structure
tasks.prompt: Create a minimal pyproject.toml for a Python project named calculator. Use setuptools and pytest only. Do not add placeholder authors, URLs, README references, Black, Flake8, or unrelated dependencies. Create only the files listed in this task.

events.message: Task completed after approved Nightshift cycle.

tasks.title: Implement add()
tasks.prompt: Create calculator.py containing a single function add(a: float, b: float) -> float. Create test_calculator.py with focused pytest tests for positive numbers, negative numbers and zero.

events.message: Task completed after approved Nightshift cycle.

tasks.title: Implement subtract()
tasks.prompt: Add subtract(a: float, b: float) -> float to calculator.py. Extend test_calculator.py with focused pytest tests for subtraction. Do not modify existing tests except when necessary to add new ones.

events.message: Task completed after approved Nightshift cycle.

tasks.title: Implement multiply()
tasks.prompt: Add multiply(a: float, b: float) -> float to calculator.py. Extend test_calculator.py with pytest tests covering multiplication with positive, negative and zero values.

events.message: Task completed after approved Nightshift cycle.

tasks.title: Implement divide()
tasks.prompt: Add divide(a: float, b: float) -> float to calculator.py. Extend test_calculator.py with pytest tests covering normal division and division resulting in floating point values.

events.message: Task completed after approved Nightshift cycle.

tasks.title: Handle division by zero
tasks.prompt: Modify divide() so that division by zero raises ValueError with a clear error message. Extend test_calculator.py with focused pytest tests verifying the exception.

events.message: Task completed after approved Nightshift cycle.

tasks.title: Create calculator CLI
tasks.prompt: Create main.py that provides a minimal command-line interface. When started it should print 'Calculator v1.0'. Do not implement expression parsing yet. Add focused pytest tests verifying the CLI output.

events.message: Task completed after approved Nightshift cycle.

tasks.title: Parse command-line arguments
tasks.prompt: Extend main.py to accept three command-line arguments: operation, operand1 and operand2. Supported operations are add, subtract, multiply and divide. The CLI should call the corresponding calculator function. Add focused pytest tests.

tasks.title: Handle invalid operations
tasks.prompt: Update the CLI to detect unsupported operations. Print a clear error message and return a non-zero exit code. Add focused pytest tests.

tasks.title: Handle invalid numeric input
tasks.prompt: Update the CLI so invalid numeric input is handled gracefully. Print a clear error message instead of raising an unhandled exception. Add focused pytest tests.

tasks.title: Add usage help
tasks.prompt: Implement a --help option describing the supported operations and expected arguments. Add focused pytest tests verifying the help text.

tasks.title: Add version option
tasks.prompt: Implement a --version option that prints the application version. The version should match the version defined in pyproject.toml. Add focused pytest tests.

tasks.title: Improve documentation
tasks.prompt: Create a README.md describing installation, available operations and example command-line usage. Keep the documentation concise.

tasks.title: Final project cleanup
tasks.prompt: Review the project for consistency. Improve docstrings, type hints and formatting where appropriate without changing behaviour. Ensure all tests still pass and avoid unnecessary refactoring.
